### Post by macvr on 2008-11-01
hi all...**_ i just upgraded to 8.10 from 8.04_** , i'm not able to start ubuntu in normal mode... it just stops saying
>  COULD NOT START THE X SERVER DUE TO SOME INTERNAL ERROR. PLS CONTACT ADMIN or check your system log to diagnose .
in the mean time this display will be disabled..... how do i correct this?
my xorg.conf file```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

#

```

i also have a problem where at startup the system waits **5mins** to continue with the booting, it stops at this line
> start-stop-daemon:Unable to open pidfile '/var/run/klogd/kmsgpipe.pid' for writing : read only file system (reda only file system) 
and after 5mins it says> [[COLOR="Red"]failed[/COLOR]] and continues to boot where it stops at the x server error and shows just the command prompt.


***previously** i had a gdm problem which gave this error>* i'm not sure if this has anything to do with the present error, but just mentioning
> server authorization directory is set to /var/lib/gdm but is not owned by the user 106 & group 114. please correct the ownership or GDM configuration & restart GDM
i did a 
```
sudo apt-get autoremove gdm --purge
sudo apt-get install gdm
```
which i think has corrected the GDM problem.

how do i correct the X server problem?
i'm using an ATI X1400 graphics card on my laptop and also not able to activate the restricted drivers ,fglrx.

Try ```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

### Post by phidia on 2008-11-01
Sometimes an upgrade doesn't work correctly and a claen install is required-I'm not saying that's the case here, but it could be.

You can also keep an eye on [ibex bug reports]("https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/intrepid/+bugs?search=Search&field.status=New") or make a report.

### Post by phidia on 2008-11-01
Welcome to Ubuntu hornedOwl93,  You can try this command copy and paste it if you can > sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

That will back up your old xorg (but it's not working now anyway) and create a new one.

How did you install the ati driver in hardy? Chances are the old driver is there and could be causing problems.

If you really want to keep working with this rather than do a clean install you could also try the driver directly available from the [ati site]("http://ati.amd.com/support/driver.html").

### Post by marko_4454 on 2008-11-06
I had this problem and this is how I solved it. I had tried pretty much everything, and it turned out to be the simplest thing. 
I just went to the Hardware Drivers Restricted Manager (something like that, im not in my ubuntu box) and disabled the driver (ATI). I then restarted and went to the same menu and activated.
I have to point out that this worked and I had tried reinstalling the ATI driver from ati.com website and it didnt work. The procedure above worked though.

---

---

### Post by callmesid on 2008-11-07
I was seeing the same error as reported in the original post. After messing around with the boot options within grub by editing /boot/grub/menu.lst, I found that removing the 'splash' option from the kernel line did the trick for me.

title		Ubuntu 8.10, kernel 2.6.27-7-generic
root		(hd0,0)
kernel		/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.27-7-generic root=UUID=fa27a539-3cb3-48f3-b77a
-e4ddbd1053ef ro quiet [COLOR="Red"]splash[/COLOR]
initrd		/boot/initrd.img-2.6.27-7-generic
quiet

'splash' triggers the progress bar visualization. I think this program does not work correctly after upgrading from Hardy to Ibex.

So the current 'solution' / workaround options are:
1. Get rid of the 'splash' option as described above. 
This will result in the GUI progress bar visualization going away. 

2. If you do like the progress bar visualization, here is a replacement to the default bootsplash program

[http://www.ubuntugeek.com/how-to-change-the-bootsplash-in-ubuntu-810-intrepid-ibex.html](http://www.ubuntugeek.com/how-to-change-the-bootsplash-in-ubuntu-810-intrepid-ibex.html)

---

title		Ubuntu 8.10, kernel 2.6.27-7-generic
root		(hd0,0)
kernel		/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.27-7-generic root=UUID=fa27a539-3cb3-48f3-b77a
-e4ddbd1053ef ro quiet [COLOR="Red"]splash[/COLOR]
initrd		/boot/initrd.img-2.6.27-7-generic
quiet

'splash' triggers the progress bar visualization. I think this program does not work correctly after upgrading from Hardy to Ibex.

So the current 'solution' / workaround options are:
1. Get rid of the 'splash' option as described above. 
This will result in the GUI progress bar visualization going away. 

2. If you do like the progress bar visualization, here is a replacement to the default bootsplash program

[http://www.ubuntugeek.com/how-to-change-the-bootsplash-in-ubuntu-810-intrepid-ibex.html](http://www.ubuntugeek.com/how-to-change-the-bootsplash-in-ubuntu-810-intrepid-ibex.html)

worked for me too... so the splash has been causing the problems, i tried reinstalling all the 3 packages used by usplash but still had same problem, if i used splash...

so for now omitting SPLASH works for me :)
